CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 64252536) FILED BY PLAINTIFF TOMSIC, JUDITH J. JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-21-2021 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WCP I DE, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS ATTENDANCE AT AN INDEPENDENT MENTAL EXAMINATION, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS. BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS PUT HER MENTAL CONDITION AT ISSUE, DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO TAKE A PSYCHOLOGICAL IME. HOWEVER, REQUIRING THE PLAINTIFF TO TRAVEL FROM HER (PRESUMABLY) NORTH BAY HOME TO LOS GATOS AND THEN TO SUBMIT TO AN ALL DAY EXAMINATION AND THEN RETURN HOME SEEMS EXCESSIVE. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH TO ATTEMPT TO LIMIT THE ENTIRE EXAM IME PROCESS TO APPROXIMATELY FOUR HOURS PLUS TRAVEL TIME. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-13-2021 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2021 Continued To Sep-28-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-30-2021 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-27-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WCP I DE, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's summary judgment motion in this slip-and-fall case is denied. Defendant's notice of motion fails to state its grounds (CRC 3.1112), so this order addresses the three arguments made in its memorandum's introduction. Triable issues exist as to each of the three.

First, defendant says the "claimed 1/2" height differential does not constitute a 'dangerous condition' as a matter of law." However, the opinions cited do not address the situation here: a depression in paving stones next to a concrete strip.

Second, defendant's employee testified to inspecting the walkway "almost on a daily basis," raising a dispute as to whether defendant had actual and/or constructive notice. (Ortega v. Kmart Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1200, 1206; Szeto Dep. 16, 21-22.) Also disputed is defendant's claim that there were "no complaints" regarding the walkway. (Price Dep. 53, 68-69, 74.)

Third, defendant argues that plaintiff herself was negligent, but that would not absolve defendant of liability. (American Motorcycle Ass'n v. Sup. Ct. (1978) 20 Cal.3d 578.)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-04-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Attendance At An Independent Mental Examination; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-21-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WCP I DE, LLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Attendance At An Independent Mental Examination.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The Motion is granted as follows. Because Plaintiff has put her mental condition at issue, Defendants are entitled to take a psychological IME. However, requiring the Plaintiff to travel from her (presumably) North Bay home to Los Gatos and then to submit to an all day examination and then return home seems excessive. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in good faith to attempt to limit the entire exam IME process to approximately four hours plus travel time.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
